architecture_fit.note: Single hand-built head + one gating nonlinearity; the (1+cos)-corrected magnitude S exactly recovers the feature count, solving AND with sharpness ~0.996 across the full sweep.

baseline_comparison.note: Overlays the goal's linear baseline plus two ablations (drop-gate, per-feature product) and shows the product gate collapsing at cos=1 where the magnitude head holds.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic hand-set circuit (no model to patch); README says so explicitly and proposes a trained-head ablation, with within-mechanism ablations standing in for causal evidence.

operating_range.note: Fully sweeps the goal's relevant axis cos∈[0,1] with robustness 1.0, but does not probe other scale axes (feature density, noise magnitude, d).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set weights — the mechanism is derived in closed form and reproduces the target behaviour without any training.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab shows sharpness-vs-cosine with all four lines (flat top vs diving product gate) plus a labelled S-separation scatter with the threshold drawn — claim is legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies y=sharpness, x=cosine, the baseline/ablation overlays, and the second panel zooming into why S separates the 'both' cluster.

notes: An elegant hand-built circuit that thresholds the cosine-corrected total feature magnitude — algebraically equal to the feature count — to implement AND, achieving ~0.996 sharpness and perfect superposition_robustness (1.0) across the whole cosine sweep. Baselines and two pointed ablations make the contrast explicit, and the visualisation cleanly conveys both the robustness curve and the underlying separation. Only ceilings are the inherent lack of a trained-model causal check (acknowledged) and no exploration of scale axes beyond cosine.
